meaning: Breathing with a whistling or rattling sound in the chest.
Intense tightening in the chest, air hunger, difficulty breathing, breathlessness.
A noticeably rapid, strong, or irregular heartbeat due to agitation, exertion, or illness.
An involuntary quivering movement.
Exuding sweat as a result of being too hot, ill, or afraid.
The state of being numb; a lack of sensation.
Experience or cause to experience a slight prickling or stinging sensation.
A swollen state caused by retention of fluid or gas.
An involuntary spasm of the diaphragm and respiratory organs.
Habitual sleeplessness; inability to sleep.
A sensation of spinning around and losing one's balance.
Extreme tiredness resulting from mental or physical exertion or illness.
A feeling of coldness occurring during a high fever.
Make a sudden involuntary expulsion of air from the nose and mouth due to irritation of one's nostrils.
The blockage of the nasal passages usually due to membranes lining the nose becoming swollen.
The thick viscous substance secreted by the mucous membranes of the respiratory passages.
A feeling of sickness with an inclination to vomit.
Eject matter from the stomach through the mouth.
An area of reddening of a person's skin, sometimes with raised spots.
Have or cause an uncomfortable sensation on the skin that makes one want to scratch.
A bluish discoloration of the skin resulting from poor circulation or inadequate oxygenation of the blood.
The condition of lacking the power of hearing or having impaired hearing.
The state or condition of being unable to see because of injury, disease, or a congenital condition.
An acutely disturbed state of mind that occurs in fever, intoxication, and other disorders.
A state of deep unconsciousness that lasts for a prolonged or indefinite period.
A sudden, violent, irregular movement of a limb or of the body.
A sudden attack of illness, especially a stroke or an epileptic fit.
Ringing or buzzing in the ears.
A sensation of whirling and loss of balance.
The accumulation of gas in the alimentary canal.
